headline: Core governance and architecture identified; specific deployed program addresses require access to pinned documentation

11-member governance multisig identified with members: Jito, Jupiter, Laine, Mango, MRGN, Solblaze, SolanaFM, Sanctum

rationale.verdict: D2 deployment documentation inaccessible; D3 audit reports not linked; D4 GitHub repository not pinned. Website confirms governance multisig structure and auditors but specific program addresses cannot be enumerated without access to deployed contracts documentation. Recorded as off-chain-only with unknowns blocking address discovery.

evidence.shows: Main protocol website confirming Sanctum as leading LST infrastructure on Solana; references Infinity pool, validator LSTs, and governance via CLOUD token
Blog archive documenting governance structure, audits, and deployed LST programs including references to deployed programs documentation at learn.sanctum.so

Technical documentation confirming 11-member multisig governance (Jito, Jupiter, Laine, Mango, MRGN, Solblaze, SolanaFM, Sanctum), audits by Halborn, Kudelski, Ottersec, Neodyme, Quantstamp; fork of Solana Labs stake pool program v1.3.4

protocol_metadata.about: Sanctum is Solana's leading liquid staking infrastructure protocol, enabling users to stake SOL and receive liquid staking tokens (LSTs) representing their stake with validators. The protocol powers over 1,300 LSTs including JupSOL, bbSOL, and hundreds of validator-specific LSTs. Sanctum provides unified liquidity through its Infinity pool and instant unstaking through its Reserve mechanism, allowing users to access their capital while continuing to earn staking rewards. Governance is managed by an 11-member multisig comprising ecosystem leaders including Jito, Jupiter, Laine, Mango, MRGN, Solblaze, SolanaFM, and Sanctum.
